name: python3-semantic-version
title: python3-semantic-version
description:
implementing the SemVer scheme - Python 3.x
This small Python library provides a few tools to handle SemVer in Python. It
follows strictly the 2.0.0 version of the SemVer scheme. semantic_version can
also support versions which wouldn't match the semantic version scheme by
converting a version such as 0.1.2.3.4 into 0.1.2+3.4.
.
This package contains the Python 3.x module.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use python3-semantic-version because implementing the SemVer scheme - Python 3.x.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use python3-semantic-version as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair python3-semantic-version with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `python3-semantic-version --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
